svg.title: Network granularity comparison: per sub-domain vs per domain
svg.desc: Two side-by-side architectures showing the same AI workloads grouped by sub-domain (11 networks) versus by domain (4 networks plus shared tiers). Per-domain shows fewer networks and shared infrastructure.
svg.text: 11 sub-domains across ml, dl, nlp, agentic
each with its own mlflow, postgres, minio
11 copies of mlflow (or shared via multi-attach)
~33 GB RAM just for infra services
Each sub-domain feels like its own product
Realistic match to enterprise multi-tenant SaaS
Heavy ops burden for one operator

g.text: Option B: per domain + shared tiers
Routes by hostname to domain networks
All domain apps connect via multi-attach

svg.text: Sub-domains see each other within their domain
Matches how real MLOps teams organize
Single operator can run this comfortably
Easy to split a sub-domain out later if needed
